div.head: 125. Telegram From the Embassy in Poland to the Department of StateSource: Department of State, Central Files, 611.93/9–2558. Secret; Niact; Limit Distribution. Received at 12:42 p.m. Repeated priority to USUN and niact Taipei.
div.p:
475. USUN for Lodge. Beam–Wang Talks. Today’s meeting one hour 45 minutes.Beam sent a detailed report of the meeting in telegram 477 from Warsaw, September 25. (Ibid.; see Supplement) Wang asked lead off to which I agreed and he read statement covering same familiar ground he covered last meeting, adamantly rejecting ceasefire, accusing US of ulterior motives and insisting on US withdrawal forces from area could relieve tension, no significant alternative tone or language from that adopted last meeting. I responded with brief rebuttal several specific points, then read prepared statement based Department’s 329.Document 123. Wang made vigorous rebuttal but said nothing new. Brief further exchange brought meeting to close. I did not mention we would present new draft next meeting as there seemed no necessity tip
our hand. Wang proposed Monday afternoon. I countered with Monday morning as more convenient and we agreed on Tuesday September 30, 10 a.m.
